markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spell Duelist's Siphon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2182)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 113

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Metal clasps line the spine of this book, and diagrams displaying proper somatic casting forms are etched into its cover.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You're targeted with an [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11) spell attack and you have this grimoire raised; **Requirements** You have the [Raise a Tome](/Feats.aspx?ID=2848) feat; **Effect** The grimoire attempts to absorb knowledge of the spell targeting you. You attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the triggering spell. If you succeed, the spell is absorbed into the grimoire, and the diagrams on the cover change to indicate the somatic gestures and sigils for the counteracted spell. While the grimoire contains a spell, you can spend a spell slot of the same or higher rank as the spell in the grimoire to cast that spell instead, heightened to the appropriate rank (if you spent a higher-rank spell slot). After you cast the spell, it’s expended from the grimoire.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spell Duelist's Siphon

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tome of Scintillating Sleet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2184)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 113

**Price** 430 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The first 12 pages of this tome tell the same story in two languages: 6 pages in [Skald](/Languages.aspx?ID=42) and 6 pages in the ancient [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) dialect used by saumen kar, a species of ice-dwelling [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91). The story is a tale of a saumen kar stricken with snow blindness after spending too long under the sun building snow giants.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast a [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) spell that deals damage, all creatures damaged by the spell are also [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 3 rounds by light refracting and reflecting within and around the spell's chilling effects. If an affected creature critically failed its save against the required spell, or if you critically succeeded on your spell attack roll against the creature, it's instead [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round and then dazzled for 3 rounds.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tome of Scintillating Sleet

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Undertaker's Manifest](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2185)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Darkness" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=39" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 113

**Price** 220 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This grim collection of spreadsheets is used both by undertakers who occasionally need to avoid the notice of their more restless clients, and by industrious necromancers looking to avoid catching the notice of cemetery guards and vigilant undertakers.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast a [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693) or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) spell, the spell’s casting is accompanied by a roiling cloud of shadow that spills out around you, creating [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) centered on you for the next 3 rounds. This has no effect on areas where the lighting level is already darker than dim light.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Undertaker's Manifest

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Anchor of Aquatic Exploration](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2186)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 114

**Price** 690 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

No matter how often it's washed, the last crystals of salt rime never quite leave this pitted anchor. When you're holding the _anchor of aquatic exploration_, you can breathe underwater; however, you can't [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39), losing any Swim speed you have, automatically failing any [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to Swim, and so on. Instead, when you enter a body of water, you sink to the bottom at a rate of 25 feet per round and can move across the bottom at your normal Speed. You're protected from environmental effects of deep water such as pressure, cold temperatures, and any negative consequences of depressurization.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You're underwater; **Effect** You spend one minute digging the anchor into the seabed, after which the anchor casts a [_cozy cabin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=681) spell, summoning a sunken ship instead of a cabin. The sunken ship is filled with breathable air.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Anchor of Aquatic Exploration

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Battle Medic's Baton](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2187)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 114

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This short bronze rod has the form of a serpent coiled around it. While you hold it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You have the [Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=760) action; **Effect** You use Battle Medicine. The target is temporarily immune to your Battle Medicine for 1 hour instead of 1 day.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Battle Medic's Baton

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bring Me Near](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2188)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 114

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This collapsible [fine spyglass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=54) consists of 3 leather tubes that slide into one another. The edge of each is trimmed in silver, and the lenses are made of finely crafted glass. While looking through it, you gain a +2 item bonus to any Perception checks made involving sight.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You focus on any spot you can see within 5 miles through the spyglass and rotate its parts in a meticulous order. You and up to 4 willing creatures adjacent to you are instantly teleported to that spot. If there's not enough room for everyone, only you are transported. If there's not enough room for you, the teleportation fails.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bring Me Near

<additional-info>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 19"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cube of Recall](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2189)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 114

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small cube has smooth matte sides. One side is black, the opposite side is white, and the other four are various shades of gray. Each side can be attuned to a location and then teleport you back to that spot in the blink of an eye.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** Pick one side of the cube and set it face up. You attune the cube to the location you currently occupy. Each side can be attuned to only one location. Once you use this action to attune to a location, the side you pick loses any previously attuned location.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710))    **Effect** While speaking a word of command and bringing the location into your mind, you push the corresponding side of the cube. You teleport to the location attuned to the side you press, within 100 feet of the attuned location, as long as that location is on the same planet. If it's not, your activation produces no effect, but the attunement remains.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cube of Recall

This coin is struck with the image of a beatific seraph in gold on one side and a fearsome fiend with seven eyes enameled in black on the other. While it may seem nothing more than a curiosity, it's a powerful agent of fortune when activated.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))  **Trigger** You fail a check or attack;  **Effect** Flip the coin. If it lands on the seraph side, you get a 12 on the die instead of what you rolled. If it lands on the fiend side, one of the eyes on the fiend closes. Either way, you're temporarily immune to _fortune's coin_ for 1 hour. When all seven eyes are closed, the coin vanishes into a puff of smoke, disappearing forever. This activation is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect, regardless of how the coin flip lands.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghost Lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2191)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 115

**Price** 1,850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crafted from [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916), this black hooded [lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2730) has gray glass and lenses that emit a pale gray light when the lantern is lit. Anything this light falls on looks gray or desaturated. The lantern uses oil as a standard hooded lantern. The lantern’s light shines within the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) as well as [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). On any other plane, the light functions as a normal hooded lantern.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The lantern's shutters are open; **Effect** You concentrate on the lantern’s light to soften the boundary between the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) and [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). Any creature in the lantern’s bright light on the Universe gains the effects of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune on all its weapons and unarmed attacks. If an affected weapon or attack is [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) and already has the maximum number of property runes, the wielder can choose one to suppress to gain ghost touch. This benefit lasts for 5 minutes or until the shutters are closed, whichever comes first. It applies to a creature only while it’s in the lantern’s [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402), and if the creature leaves the light and returns it regains the benefit once more.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghost Lantern

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Horrid Figurine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2192)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 115

**Price** 450 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved in a putrid jade, the color of disease, this figurine is a bloated humanoid mass of writhing vermin and serpents, all rendered in disgusting detail. The creeping pattern is carved so they seem to move and contort the more one views the figurine. When activated, the effect is amplified to a disgusting or horrifying degree. The figurine can be activated twice per day. If you try to activate it a third time during a day, it dissolves into a puddle of non-magical, putrid glue, causing you to become [sickened 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** Holding the figurine over your head and speaking one command word causes a wave of nausea in a 20-foot emanation. Each creature in the emanation must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or become sickened 2. You're immune to this effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** Holding the figurine over your head and speaking a different command word causes those around to tremble in fear. Each creature in a 20-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 24 Will save or become [frightened 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19). You're immune to this effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Horrid Figurine

    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Jug of Fond Remembrance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2193)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 115

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This large jug always seems to contain just enough of the holder's favorite alcohol to share with a friend. As long as you're holding the jug, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks. If you share a sip of the liquor from the jug with a creature, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your next Diplomacy check to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) something from that creature any time within the next month.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You take a long swig on the jug and then [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about a creature you can see, with a +2 circumstance bonus to the check. If you fail but don't critically fail this check, you get a success instead. You're then [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 3 rounds.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Jug of Fond Remembrance

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Legerdemain Handkerchief](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2194)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 115

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This frilled, silvery-gray handkerchief is a stylish tool for personal hygiene by all appearances, but it can be used to covertly make very small items vanish.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** The handkerchief is entirely covering an item of negligible Bulk; **Effect** The handkerchief transports the item it covers into its extradimensional space. The handkerchief can hold only one item within its extradimensional space at a time, so any item taken is replaced by any item already within the space. You can also use this action to expel an item already within the extradimensional space without replacing it. This activation can't be used on an attended item unless the creature with that item allows it. Placing the handkerchief over an item typically takes an Interact action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Legerdemain Handkerchief

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Planar Tunnel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2195)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 115

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A complex, collapsible mechanism forms the outer surface of a _planar tunnel_. It’s linked to a passageway that can be accessed if the tunnel is fully opened. The passage is [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=596), but borders another plane of existence. Each planar tunnel is keyed to one specific plane.

If another extradimensional space is opened inside the planar tunnel, rather than deactivating either item as typical for extradimensional spaces, a rift to the keyed plane tears open and destroys both items. Any creature or unattended object in the planar tunnel is instantly transported to the keyed plane, though a creature can avoid this by succeeding at a DC 30 Reflex save.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You open the collapsed mechanism to fully open the tunnel, revealing an extradimensional space that stays in place. The tunnel is 6 feet across—just big enough to cover a 5-foot square—and 10 feet deep. The passage’s depth is perpendicular to the surface, so it’s most commonly placed on a floor to make a hole straight down or on a wall to create a horizontal passage through it. The atmosphere is hospitable to travel even if the keyed plane wouldn’t be.

Anyone adjacent to either edge of the tunnel can [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to collapse the opening. This closes both entrances to the extradimensional space. Any objects or creatures within the tunnel remain inside, and any that can’t fit fully inside are ejected into the nearest open space. No matter how many items are in the planar tunnel, its Bulk never changes. Items can be stowed or retrieved only while the tunnel is fully open. When the tunnel is closed, the interior remains a stable environment for 10 minutes, after which any creature or object inside is subjected to the environment of the keyed plane as it seeps through the boundary. A living creature placed inside can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) against a DC of 13. An item inside the tunnel provides no benefits unless it’s retrieved first. Anything in the tunnel can’t be detected by magic that detects only things on the same plane.

**Special** A _planar tunnel_ can be used as a planar key for the [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) spell, and has the same rarity as the key, as specified in the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Planar Tunnel

    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sextant of the Night](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2196)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 116

**Price** 95 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This finely wrought sextant is made from silver with several onyx mirrors and shades made from thin sets of crystal glass. A [fine spyglass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=54) made of silver is affixed to the frame; removing the spyglass destroys the sextant. By all appearances, the sextant shouldn't function, as the shades and mirrors are swapped, but when you look through the spyglass, you see a night sky during the day and the sun during the night, as if day and night were inverted.

 When you use the sextant, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks, which increases to a +2 item bonus when you [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=64).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sextant of the Night

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Standard of the Primeval Howl](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2197)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 116

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These standards are always constructed from uncut wood and a leather banner painted with the visage of a snarling beast—a wolf, boar, bear, lion, dragon, or similarly imposing creature. And while it might become lost on a battlefield scattered with gaudier standards, its effect bolsters those around a competent leader. When carrying this banner, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks and initiative rolls, and creatures in a 20-foot emanation also gain a +1 item bonus to initiative checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** An ally within 20 feet of you critically hits with a Strike; **Requirements** You have the [Battle Cry](/Feats.aspx?ID=759) skill feat; **Effect** You attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) the foe the Strike hit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Standard of the Primeval Howl

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vaporous Pipe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2198)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 116

**Price** 340 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hand-polished pipe is carved from oak and releases small wisps of smoke even when unlit. As long as you're holding a _vaporous pipe_, you don't take a circumstance penalty to Perception checks due to thick smoke, and you can't [suffocate](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) from smoke and heated air (such as within a [wildfire](/Rules.aspx?ID=664)). As long as you are holding the pipe, you also gain resistance to fire equal to half your level.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You draw on the pipe and then blow a massive cloud of smoke that fills a 30-foot emanation that includes your space. All creatures within the smoke cloud are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from each other and from creatures outside the smoke, though you can still see clearly within it. The smoke dissipates after 3 rounds, or after 1 round if subjected to a strong wind.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vaporous Pipe

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Violin of the Waves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2199)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 116

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This violin is crafted from finely wrought rosewood that emits a strong, but pleasant, smell of salt water and ocean life. It's engraved with images of sailors working and waves gently rolling. When you make a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check using the _violin of the waves_, you gain a +2 item bonus to the check. This bonus increases to +3 if the performer is currently aboard a ship, walking on the ocean, or otherwise immediately adjacent to ocean water.

 The violin was created to play a specific tune, one that springs instantly into the mind of anyone who so much as casually strums the instrument. It's a tune of calm waters and safety, but also of pranks played upon shipmates and revelry.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 5 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You must be aboard a ship; **Effect** You play the song. Once it's completed, the weather immediately calms to the normal as it would for the season, as [control weather](/Rituals.aspx?ID=9). For the next day, the weather remains in this state, unless affected by other magical effects. Anyone aboard the ship finds their mind wanders when performing tasks however, daydreaming of drunken revelry or other forms of entertainment, and the crew of the ship takes a –2 status penalty to skill checks to do anything other than participate in such revelry.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Violin of the Waves

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Voice from the Grave](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2200)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 117

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This onyx skull emits eerie whispers when on its own, but when held the skull enables you to speak with [spirits](/Traits.aspx?ID=149) and [haunts](/Traits.aspx?ID=88) you can see, even if you don't share a language. The voice from the grave translates anything the spirit or haunt says, using a language you understand, and translates your words for the spirit or haunt in kind. This doesn't make the target [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=18) or even cooperative, but it does enable communication. The GM determines the language in the case of a haunt. You can't communicate with mindless spirits using the skull.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The onyx skull casts a DC 27 [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) spell on one spirit or haunt you can communicate with using the skull. If you target a haunt that doesn't have a Will modifier, it automatically gets a failure on its save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Voice from the Grave

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wind at Your Back](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2201)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 117

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This object can only be described as a gray, solidified, miniature cloud that feels spongy to the touch. The cloud is incredibly soft and can be easily lifted with little effort, though its ephemeral nature requires using two hands to ensure it doesn't slip from your grasp.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You blow across the surface of the cloud, and it floats free of you and calls up a strong breeze. For the next 8 hours, it floats behind you and your companions, increasing the amount of time the group can [Hustle](/Actions.aspx?ID=515) during exploration to the lowest Constitution modifier in the group × 20 instead of × 10. You must all remain within 100 feet to get the benefit.

 If you activate the item aboard a vehicle, you instead grant the vehicle a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speeds for 8 hours. If the vehicle is powered by wind, such as a sailing ship, the bonus increases to +20 feet.

 When the 8 hours are up, the cloud stops blowing and floats back into your hands.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wind at Your Back

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ancestral Geometry](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2202)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Geometric precision and perfect angles signify that an artist with exemplary knowledge of [dwarven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) tattooing traditions created this body art. Your family's ancestral stories, recited throughout the tattooing process, bind your blood even tighter to theirs. During your daily preparations, you manifest a visitation by an ancestor—possibly via a dream, a vision, or a magical trinket left by your bedroll. Roll 2d20 and record the highest result. Then roll 1d6 and note a type of saving throw: 1–2 Fortitude, 3–4 Reflex, and 5–6 Will.

 In addition, until the next visitation, you gain a +1 item bonus to one [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill related to the ancestor, as determined by the GM. Usually, the ancestor provides a kind of knowledge they believe you'll need. For dwarven ancestors, the Lore skill is usually Architecture Lore, Engineering Lore, Genealogy Lore, Labor Lore, Mining Lore, Warfare Lore, or Lore about a dwarven deity.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You rolled a saving throw of the noted type;  **Effect** Replace the roll with the d20 roll from your ancestor's visitation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ancestral Geometry

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bewitching Bloom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2203)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While dormant, this tattoo appears to be a simple flower bud, but when activated the flower swiftly blossoms, remaining that way until the next time you make your daily preparations. These blooms are colorful, elegant representations of distinct flower species, as determined by its type.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Choose a willing ally you can see within 30 feet. The ally fills with an emotion themed to the flower and gains the benefit listed for the type of _bewitching bloom_ you have.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Amaranth)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 5,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ally feels impervious, immortal. The next time they would take damage from a hazard, an enemy's attack, or an effect created by an enemy, that damage can't reduce the ally below 1 HP. This benefit ends if unused before the start of your next turn.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Bellflower)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A need for freedom trills through your ally's blood. Until the end of that ally's next turn, they gain a +2 status bonus to rolls to recover from the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), and [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) conditions. When you Activate the bloom, the target can attempt a new save against a condition the bonus applies to, or they can use a reaction to attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) being grabbed, [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), or restrained, provided such an attempt is allowed.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Cherry Blossom)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ally understands the fleeting nature of life and accepts fate. For 1 minute, any recovery checks the ally attempts have a DC of 10 instead of 10 + the ally's [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) value. The cherry blossom's activation can target an [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) or dying ally.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Lilac)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Memories bubble to the surface of your ally's mind. The ally attempts to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) with a +2 status bonus to the check from these vibrant memories.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Lotus)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A glow envelops your ally as a sense of enlightened peace ripples out from within. The ally gains a +2 status bonus to Will saves against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects for 1 minute. This bonus increases to +3 against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Magnolia)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 220 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Your ally seems more charming, though no one can pinpoint why. They gain a +1 status bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks for 10 minutes. Once on their next turn, the ally can attempt to draw a creature's attention. To do so, the ally must spend 1 action (which has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) traits) to select a creature the ally can see and make eye contact in a way the target can see. The target must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or be [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with your ally until the end of that ally's next turn. If the save succeeds or the effect ends, the target is temporarily immune to being fascinated by any _magnolia bewitching bloom_ for 24 hours.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Purple Iris)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Your ally takes on a regal bearing, exuding the presence of confident royalty. The bloom casts a 5th-rank [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) spell (DC 28) selecting targets within range of the ally. Each target that fails the save must fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and pays homage to your ally.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Red Rose)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ally fills with feelings of love. They chooses a creature that the ally has strong positive emotions toward as beloved. While the ally is within 10 feet of this beloved, the ally gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws. If the ally starts their turn within 10 feet of the beloved, the ally gains 5 temporary Hit Points. These benefits last for 1 minute, and temporary HP remaining from this effect at the end of that time disappear. This effect has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (White Poppy)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A sense of peace overwhelms the ally. The tattoo casts [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458) or [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) (your choice) on the ally. The spell affects only the ally and lasts only until the end of your next turn. Your ally need not be willing if under the [control](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) of another creature. However, the controlling creature can attempt a DC 20 Will save, disrupting your activation on a success.
name: Bewitching Bloom

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A need for freedom trills through your ally's blood. Until the end of that ally's next turn, they gain a +2 status bonus to rolls to recover from the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), and [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) conditions. When you Activate the bloom, the target can attempt a new save against a condition the bonus applies to, or they can use a reaction to attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) being grabbed, [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), or restrained, provided such an attempt is allowed.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Cherry Blossom)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ally understands the fleeting nature of life and accepts fate. For 1 minute, any recovery checks the ally attempts have a DC of 10 instead of 10 + the ally's [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) value. The cherry blossom's activation can target an [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) or dying ally.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Lilac)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Memories bubble to the surface of your ally's mind. The ally attempts to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) with a +2 status bonus to the check from these vibrant memories.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Lotus)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A glow envelops your ally as a sense of enlightened peace ripples out from within. The ally gains a +2 status bonus to Will saves against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects for 1 minute. This bonus increases to +3 against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Magnolia)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 220 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Your ally seems more charming, though no one can pinpoint why. They gain a +1 status bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks for 10 minutes. Once on their next turn, the ally can attempt to draw a creature's attention. To do so, the ally must spend 1 action (which has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) traits) to select a creature the ally can see and make eye contact in a way the target can see. The target must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or be [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with your ally until the end of that ally's next turn. If the save succeeds or the effect ends, the target is temporarily immune to being fascinated by any _magnolia bewitching bloom_ for 24 hours.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Purple Iris)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Your ally takes on a regal bearing, exuding the presence of confident royalty. The bloom casts a 5th-rank [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) spell (DC 28) selecting targets within range of the ally. Each target that fails the save must fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and pays homage to your ally.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Red Rose)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ally fills with feelings of love. They chooses a creature that the ally has strong positive emotions toward as beloved. While the ally is within 10 feet of this beloved, the ally gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws. If the ally starts their turn within 10 feet of the beloved, the ally gains 5 temporary Hit Points. These benefits last for 1 minute, and temporary HP remaining from this effect at the end of that time disappear. This effect has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (White Poppy)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A sense of peace overwhelms the ally. The tattoo casts [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458) or [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) (your choice) on the ally. The spell affects only the ally and lasts only until the end of your next turn. Your ally need not be willing if under the [control](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) of another creature. However, the controlling creature can attempt a DC 20 Will save, disrupting your activation on a success.
name: Bewitching Bloom (Amaranth)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While dormant, this tattoo appears to be a simple flower bud, but when activated the flower swiftly blossoms, remaining that way until the next time you make your daily preparations. These blooms are colorful, elegant representations of distinct flower species, as determined by its type.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Choose a willing ally you can see within 30 feet. The ally fills with an emotion themed to the flower and gains the benefit listed for the type of _bewitching bloom_ you have.

right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Bellflower)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A need for freedom trills through your ally's blood. Until the end of that ally's next turn, they gain a +2 status bonus to rolls to recover from the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), and [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) conditions. When you Activate the bloom, the target can attempt a new save against a condition the bonus applies to, or they can use a reaction to attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) being grabbed, [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), or restrained, provided such an attempt is allowed.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Cherry Blossom)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ally understands the fleeting nature of life and accepts fate. For 1 minute, any recovery checks the ally attempts have a DC of 10 instead of 10 + the ally's [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) value. The cherry blossom's activation can target an [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) or dying ally.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Lilac)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Memories bubble to the surface of your ally's mind. The ally attempts to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) with a +2 status bonus to the check from these vibrant memories.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Lotus)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A glow envelops your ally as a sense of enlightened peace ripples out from within. The ally gains a +2 status bonus to Will saves against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects for 1 minute. This bonus increases to +3 against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Magnolia)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 220 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Your ally seems more charming, though no one can pinpoint why. They gain a +1 status bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks for 10 minutes. Once on their next turn, the ally can attempt to draw a creature's attention. To do so, the ally must spend 1 action (which has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) traits) to select a creature the ally can see and make eye contact in a way the target can see. The target must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or be [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with your ally until the end of that ally's next turn. If the save succeeds or the effect ends, the target is temporarily immune to being fascinated by any _magnolia bewitching bloom_ for 24 hours.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Purple Iris)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Your ally takes on a regal bearing, exuding the presence of confident royalty. The bloom casts a 5th-rank [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) spell (DC 28) selecting targets within range of the ally. Each target that fails the save must fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and pays homage to your ally.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Red Rose)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ally fills with feelings of love. They chooses a creature that the ally has strong positive emotions toward as beloved. While the ally is within 10 feet of this beloved, the ally gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws. If the ally starts their turn within 10 feet of the beloved, the ally gains 5 temporary Hit Points. These benefits last for 1 minute, and temporary HP remaining from this effect at the end of that time disappear. This effect has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (White Poppy)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A sense of peace overwhelms the ally. The tattoo casts [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458) or [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) (your choice) on the ally. The spell affects only the ally and lasts only until the end of your next turn. Your ally need not be willing if under the [control](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) of another creature. However, the controlling creature can attempt a DC 20 Will save, disrupting your activation on a success.
name: Bewitching Bloom (Lilac)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bewitching Bloom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2203)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While dormant, this tattoo appears to be a simple flower bud, but when activated the flower swiftly blossoms, remaining that way until the next time you make your daily preparations. These blooms are colorful, elegant representations of distinct flower species, as determined by its type.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Choose a willing ally you can see within 30 feet. The ally fills with an emotion themed to the flower and gains the benefit listed for the type of _bewitching bloom_ you have.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</column>

---

The ally feels impervious, immortal. The next time they would take damage from a hazard, an enemy's attack, or an effect created by an enemy, that damage can't reduce the ally below 1 HP. This benefit ends if unused before the start of your next turn.

The ally feels impervious, immortal. The next time they would take damage from a hazard, an enemy's attack, or an effect created by an enemy, that damage can't reduce the ally below 1 HP. This benefit ends if unused before the start of your next turn.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Bellflower)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A need for freedom trills through your ally's blood. Until the end of that ally's next turn, they gain a +2 status bonus to rolls to recover from the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), and [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) conditions. When you Activate the bloom, the target can attempt a new save against a condition the bonus applies to, or they can use a reaction to attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) being grabbed, [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), or restrained, provided such an attempt is allowed.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Cherry Blossom)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ally understands the fleeting nature of life and accepts fate. For 1 minute, any recovery checks the ally attempts have a DC of 10 instead of 10 + the ally's [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) value. The cherry blossom's activation can target an [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) or dying ally.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Lilac)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Memories bubble to the surface of your ally's mind. The ally attempts to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) with a +2 status bonus to the check from these vibrant memories.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Lotus)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A glow envelops your ally as a sense of enlightened peace ripples out from within. The ally gains a +2 status bonus to Will saves against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects for 1 minute. This bonus increases to +3 against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Magnolia)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 220 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Your ally seems more charming, though no one can pinpoint why. They gain a +1 status bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks for 10 minutes. Once on their next turn, the ally can attempt to draw a creature's attention. To do so, the ally must spend 1 action (which has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) traits) to select a creature the ally can see and make eye contact in a way the target can see. The target must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or be [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with your ally until the end of that ally's next turn. If the save succeeds or the effect ends, the target is temporarily immune to being fascinated by any _magnolia bewitching bloom_ for 24 hours.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Purple Iris)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Your ally takes on a regal bearing, exuding the presence of confident royalty. The bloom casts a 5th-rank [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) spell (DC 28) selecting targets within range of the ally. Each target that fails the save must fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and pays homage to your ally.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (Red Rose)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ally fills with feelings of love. They chooses a creature that the ally has strong positive emotions toward as beloved. While the ally is within 10 feet of this beloved, the ally gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws. If the ally starts their turn within 10 feet of the beloved, the ally gains 5 temporary Hit Points. These benefits last for 1 minute, and temporary HP remaining from this effect at the end of that time disappear. This effect has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bewitching Bloom (White Poppy)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A sense of peace overwhelms the ally. The tattoo casts [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458) or [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) (your choice) on the ally. The spell affects only the ally and lasts only until the end of your next turn. Your ally need not be willing if under the [control](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) of another creature. However, the controlling creature can attempt a DC 20 Will save, disrupting your activation on a success.
name: Bewitching Bloom (Purple Iris)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Boozy Bottle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2204)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 119

**Price** 140 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo depicts a container of alcohol, traditionally a small, uncorked brown bottle. You gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail (but don't critically fail) an initial saving throw against a poison, or you gain [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29);  **Effect** You pick your poison. Calling out the name of a drink as though ordering at a bar, you negate the triggering poison. Instead, you become slightly drunk. For 10 minutes you’re [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) and gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fauna Guardian](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2205)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 120

**Price** 675 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _fauna guardian_ is a tattoo of an animal, which you can temporarily animate to protect you. The animal must be chosen when you get the tattoo, and can be any [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) of 4th level or lower. (Your GM might allow other options.)

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You animate your tattoo using the duration and other parameters of a 5th-rank [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1694) spell. It appears in a space adjacent to you. If your tattoo animal drops to 0 HP, the activation ends, and the inanimate tattoo returns to your skin.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fauna Guardian

    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eye Slash](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2206)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 120

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Small scars or marks around your eye improve your distant vision. These scars are especially common among [orc](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=19) scouts, who favor scars shaped like eagle talons. You can see four times farther than normal. If you have [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415), you can see blood in color. Higher-level versions of an _eye slash_ are larger and more elaborate scars or marks, radiating out around the eye.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Faith Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2207)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 120

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You have marked your body to show your devotion to a deity. This tattoo could be the deity's religious symbol, another image that evokes that deity, or another mark you gained through your devotion. The tattoo serves as a silver [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of the deity. Provided you keep the tattoo uncovered, you need not wield it to gain that benefit.

When you get the tattoo and aren’t sanctified, you can choose to [sanctify](/Classes.aspx?ID=33) yourself to your deity.

 If you cease meeting the prerequisites, the tattoo fades, and you lose its benefits until you perform an [_atone_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2) ritual and meet the prerequisites thereafter.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), or the 1st-rank spell from your deity’s cleric spells. You can choose _harm_ or _heal_ only in accord with the deity’s divine font. If the deity allows either spell, choose one the tattoo can cast when you receive the tattoo. The DC for any of these spells is 18.

**Craft Requirements** silver worth 2 gp to include in the ink

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>

<traits>

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), or the 1st-rank spell from your deity’s cleric spells. You can choose _harm_ or _heal_ only in accord with the deity’s divine font. If the deity allows either spell, choose one the tattoo can cast when you receive the tattoo. The DC for any of these spells is 18.

**Craft Requirements** silver worth 2 gp to include in the ink

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Frostwalker Pattern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2208)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 120

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Northern peoples design magical tattoos to protect against wintry weather, typically in geometric patterns with a combination of straight lines and whorls. This tattoo negates any damage you take from [severe environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) and reduces damage you take from extreme cold to equal that of severe cold.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Until the end of your next turn, you ignore difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain from ice and snow and don't risk falling when crossing ice.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Frostwalker Pattern

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Homeward Swallow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2209)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 120

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small tattoo of a swallow always points toward your home. The tattooing must take place at a location you consider to be your home, or the magic fails to bind with the ink. When you travel to your home using [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) that can be off target, such as [_teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720) or [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), you arrive exactly at your home. If your home is destroyed or you come to believe a new place is your home, this tattoo fades from your skin.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** You sense the direction toward your home.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Homeward Swallow

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Memoir Map](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2210)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 120

**Price** 13 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your journeys and the major events in your life that occur after you obtain this tattoo appear on it, your life story traced upon your skin. Each time you journey somewhere new or accomplish something noteworthy to you, a design or symbol appears, representing the event. The positions of these images are relative in location, but measurements aren't exact. A _memoir map_ starts with an icon representing your location when you receive the tattoo, usually over the heart, and grows from there. Traveling to another plane causes a new portion to appear on a different part of your body to represent that plane. If you want a record of your life before you receive your _memoir map_, you can have the tattoo artist embellish the map to represent past events.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Memoir Map

    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mortal Chronicle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2211)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 121

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Common among fatalists and adventurers with access to resurrection magic, mortal chronicles look like tombstones, funeral plaques, or simple scrolls bearing the wearer's name or nickname. If you die, the date and cause of your death appear on the tattoo. The cause is literal and inexact, failing to identify specifics; it could read “beheaded” or “immolated” but not “beheaded by Amiri” or “murdered with fire.” If you're raised from the dead, a mark on the tattoo indicates the date you reversed your death. The tattoo then expands enough to list your next death when it comes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mortal Chronicle

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 121

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You want revenge badly enough that you tattoo your nemesis's name in a place you can easily see to remember your vow to settle the score. Anyone who sees the tattoo senses your hatred of the named being. Receiving a new _nemesis name_ makes any you already have non-magical.

You gain a +2 status bonus to damage rolls against the creature named on your tattoo, but this nemesis gains a +1 status bonus to damage rolls against you.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round;  **Requirements** You can see your nemesis, and they're within 30 feet of you; **Effect** You focus your hatred into a mental scream. Your nemesis takes 3d6 mental damage, which they can resist with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) DC 26 Will save. You take half as much damage as your nemesis does, and you can't reduce this damage in any way.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Nemesis Name

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[One Hundred Victories](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2214)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 121

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

One hundred cuts, healed into diamond-shaped scars, represent the ability to withstand the attacks of your enemies. [Orc](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=19) warriors covet these scar patterns and cluster them around what they consider to be their strongest assets—a pattern around the heart signifies a warrior with great endurance, while one along the arms indicates great upper body strength. 

You gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks. If you have [Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=16) (such as from the [Orc Ferocity](/Feats.aspx?ID=83) feat or a similar ability), the first time each day you use it, instead of remaining at 1 Hit Point, your Hit Points are set to an amount equal to your ancestry Hit Points, even if this amount is more than you had before using Ferocity.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: One Hundred Victories

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reading Glyphs](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2215)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 121

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These tattoos on your knuckles look like strange glyphs in an unknown language. If you press your fingertips to text in any language, these glyphs cycle in appearance through those of various extant languages. Encrypted text causes your glyphs to turn to a recognizable “null” symbol.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You sync the tattoos with the text your fingertips are touching. By running your fingers across the text, you translate it, with glyphs on your knuckles showing the translation in a language you can read. Your tattooed glyphs can't translate encrypted or encoded text, language couched in metaphor, and the like, subject to GM discretion.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Reading Glyphs

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Staring Skull](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2217)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 122

**Price** 550 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo usually depicts a humanoid skull with staring eyes in its sockets, but any creature with two eyes and a skull is possible, such as a [wolf](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=101), [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=330), or [psychopomp](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83). When your [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) condition increases to a value that would kill you, this tattoo reduces your dying value to 1 fewer than would kill you. If a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effect would kill you—provided the effect is from a creature of 8th level or lower or a spell of 4th rank or lower—this tattoo activates and keeps you alive instead. You can benefit from this ability only once per day. Each time the tattoo prevents you from dying, one of its eyes disappears, the image now featuring either an empty socket or an eyepatch or other covering. After both eyes vanish, the tattoo becomes non-magical and no longer protects you. If you receive a new _staring skull_ tattoo, any other you have loses its staring eyes and becomes non-magical.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Staring Skull

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sun Sight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2218)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 122

**Price** 850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Placed under the eyes like rays of light, this tattoo burns away illusions with the unmerciful brilliance of the sun. You gain a +2 bonus to Perception checks that involve sight. If you're [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7), you receive a new save at the start of each of your turns to end your dazzled condition.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts a 4th-rank [_blazing bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450), with the rays emitting from your eyes. The number of actions you spend Activating the tattoo determines _blazing bolt’s_ number of rays. The tattoo also attempts to dispel each illusion on a creature hit by a ray ([counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) rank 5th, counteract modifier +19).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sun Sight

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tooth and Claw Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2219)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 122

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo resembles an animal's fangs, claws, or similar natural weapons, letting you wield such weapons and turn into the same beast. When you receive the tattoo, choose the animal from among the following: ape, bear, bull, canine, cat, deer, frog, shark, or snake. You can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) skill with animals of that kind. This tattoo is usually located on the body part or parts it's meant to transform—on the back of the hands for claws, around the mouth for jaws, on the forehead for horns, and so on.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=657))    **Effect** You gain an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack matching the tattoo for 1 minute. It has the same damage as your best unarmed attack and has the same traits. Its damage type is bludgeoning for a fist or frog's jaws; piercing for an antler, fangs, horns, or most jaws; or slashing for claws.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670))    **Effect** The tattoo casts 3rd-rank [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1440) to transform you into the animal that matches your tattoo.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tooth and Claw Tattoo
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tooth and Claw Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2219)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 122

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo resembles an animal's fangs, claws, or similar natural weapons, letting you wield such weapons and turn into the same beast. When you receive the tattoo, choose the animal from among the following: ape, bear, bull, canine, cat, deer, frog, shark, or snake. You can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) skill with animals of that kind. This tattoo is usually located on the body part or parts it's meant to transform—on the back of the hands for claws, around the mouth for jaws, on the forehead for horns, and so on.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=657))    **Effect** You gain an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack matching the tattoo for 1 minute. It has the same damage as your best unarmed attack and has the same traits. Its damage type is bludgeoning for a fist or frog's jaws; piercing for an antler, fangs, horns, or most jaws; or slashing for claws.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670))    **Effect** The tattoo casts 3rd-rank [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1440) to transform you into the animal that matches your tattoo.

</title>
